description: Konami's card-battle mega-franchise based on Kazuki Takahashi's manga. Yugi Muto solves an ancient puzzle and gains the spirit of a pharaoh — and the greatest card game ever played. One of the best-selling TCGs in history (35+ billion cards) with nine anime series, four films, and 59 official video games.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! (Toei, Season 0)
media.description: The original 1998 Toei adaptation of the early manga chapters — darker, grittier, no Duel Monsters focus. Covers the Shadow Game arcs before card dueling became the core mechanic.
media.notes: No official English release. Fansub only. Completionists and manga readers only — very different tone from everything that follows.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Monster Capsule: Breed and Battle
media.description: Pre-TCG monster-raising sim. Breed, train, and battle monster capsules. PS1 release based on the early manga's Monster Capsule mini-game.
media.notes: Japan only. Not a card game — closer to a monster sim. Duckstation/PCSX2 required.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Duel Monsters (Game Boy)
media.description: The very first card game adaptation — Japan-only Game Boy release predating the physical TCG's international launch. Simple pre-official-rules dueling.
media.notes: Japan only. Included in Early Days Collection (Switch/PC, 2025). Core: gambatte

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Duel Monsters II: Dark Duel Stories
media.description: Sequel to the first GB game with a significantly expanded card pool. Japan-only GBC release.
media.notes: Japan only. Included in Early Days Collection (2025). Core: gambatte

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Forbidden Memories
media.description: Ancient Egypt story with pre-official-rules cards. Brutally RNG-dependent grinding — infamous for requiring thousands of duels. Cult classic with a unique atmosphere.
media.notes: Core: pcsx_rearmed / Duckstation. Emulator save states make the grind tolerable.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Monster Capsule GB
media.description: GBC follow-up to the PS1 capsule game. Strategic monster battles on a grid — completely different from the card game. Japan-only until 2025.
media.notes: Japan only. First international release via Early Days Collection (2025). Core: gambatte

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Dark Duel Stories
media.description: The first Yu-Gi-Oh! game released in the West. GBC card battler covering Duelist Kingdom through early Battle City. Pre-official TCG rules.
media.notes: JP title: Duel Monsters III. Included in Early Days Collection (2025). Core: gambatte

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Duel Monsters 4: Battle of Great Duelists
media.description: Released in three versions (Yugi/Kaiba/Jonouchi Decks) with link cable support for cross-version dueling. Covers the Battle City arc. Japan-only until 2025.
media.notes: Japan only. Included in Early Days Collection (2025). Core: gambatte

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Duel Monsters (Anime / Duel Monsters)
media.description: The definitive series. Yugi and Yami Yugi face Kaiba, Pegasus, Marik, and the Orichalcos across Duelist Kingdom, Battle City, Noah's World, and Dawn of the Duel. The one that made Yu-Gi-Oh! a global phenomenon.
media.notes: 224 eps (JP). EN dub on Crunchyroll/Hulu. Season 4 (Waking the Dragons) heavily edited in dub — watch JP sub for full content.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Dungeon Dice Monsters
media.description: Dice-based dungeon-building combat based on the Duke Devlin arc. Roll dice to summon monsters and build dungeon pathways — no card game mechanics.
media.notes: Included in Early Days Collection (2025). Core: mgba

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! The Eternal Duelist Soul
media.description: Massive card pool for its era, solid TCG rules approximation, full roster of anime duelists. Considered one of the best early entries.
media.notes: JP: Duel Monsters 5 Expert 1. Included in Early Days Collection (2025). Core: mgba

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! The Duelists of the Roses
media.description: Wild alternate-history AU: Yu-Gi-Oh! characters fight the Wars of the Roses as a board-game/card hybrid with unique grid movement — completely unlike standard TCG rules.
media.notes: PCSX2 required. Not mobile-friendly. Cult favorite for its unique mechanics.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Duel Monsters 6: Expert 2
media.description: Incremental update to Eternal Duelist Soul with a larger card pool. Japan-only until 2025.
media.notes: Japan only. Included in Early Days Collection (2025). Core: mgba

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! The Sacred Cards
media.description: RPG-style retelling of Battle City. Roam Domino City and duel anime characters. Pre-official TCG ruleset with elemental affinities.
media.notes: Included in Early Days Collection (2025). Core: mgba

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! The Falsebound Kingdom
media.description: GameCube real-time strategy game. Yugi and Kaiba trapped in a virtual game must command monster armies. No card game mechanics whatsoever.
media.notes: GameCube only. Requires Dolphin emulator. Niche spinoff — completionists only.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Reshef of Destruction
media.description: Sequel to Sacred Cards. Notorious for broken difficulty, punishing AI, and a tiny starting deck. Infamously one of the most poorly received entries.
media.notes: Included in Early Days Collection (2025). Core: mgba. Skip unless doing a full franchise run.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Worldwide Edition: Stairway to the Destined Duel
media.description: One of the more accurate TCG rule implementations of its era. Large anime duelist roster with tournament modes. A step above the pre-rules games.
media.notes: Included in Early Days Collection (2025). Core: mgba

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Power of Chaos: Yugi the Destiny
media.description: PC dueling sim with 3D animated Yugi as your opponent. Official TCG rules. Part one of a trilogy (Yugi, Kaiba, Joey).
media.notes: PC only (Windows). Abandonware — no longer officially sold. Play via Sunshine or standalone.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! World Championship Tournament 2004
media.description: Accurate TCG rules simulation with large card pool and tournament ladder. One of the last GBA entries.
media.notes: JP: Duel Monsters Expert 3. Included in Early Days Collection (2025). Core: mgba

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Destiny Board Traveler
media.description: Mario Party-style board game with dueling elements. 1–4 players move across a board triggering card battles. Multiplayer party spinoff.
media.notes: JP: Sugoroku no Sugoroku. Included in Early Days Collection (2025). Core: mgba

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! The Dawn of Destiny
media.description: The only original Xbox Yu-Gi-Oh! game. Covers Battle City through Dawn of the Duel. Poorly received.
media.notes: Original Xbox only. Requires Xemu emulator. Completionists only.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Power of Chaos: Kaiba the Revenge
media.description: Second PC Power of Chaos entry. Kaiba is your opponent with his Blue-Eyes White Dragon deck.
media.notes: PC only. Abandonware.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Power of Chaos: Joey the Passion
media.description: Final PC trilogy entry. Joey Wheeler as opponent. All three Power of Chaos games were later packaged together.
media.notes: PC only. Abandonware. All three PoC games can be played together via the combined pack.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Capsule Monster Coliseum
media.description: PS2 strategic monster-combat game. Place capsule monsters on a hex grid and fight. No card mechanics.
media.notes: PCSX2 required. Developed by WinkySoft. Niche spinoff.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! 7 Trials to Glory: World Championship Tournament 2005
media.description: Final GBA World Championship entry with official TCG rules. Ends the classic GBA era.
media.notes: JP: Duel Monsters International 2. Included in Early Days Collection (2025). Core: mgba

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Online (all three versions)
media.description: Official PC online TCG service released in three iterations: Online (2005), Online: Duel Evolution (2007), Online: Duel Accelerator (2009). All servers permanently shut down.
media.notes: DEFUNCT — Duel Accelerator servers closed 2012. Historical interest only.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Nightmare Troubadour
media.description: First DS Yu-Gi-Oh! game. Duel on a virtual city map, advance through Duelist Kingdom and Battle City story beats. Good touchscreen card placement.
media.notes: Core: melonds

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! GX: Duel Academy
media.description: Last GBA entry, first GX-era game. Attend Duel Academy, duel classmates and faculty, rise through the dorm ranks.
media.notes: Core: mgba

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! GX
media.description: Jaden Yuki attends Duel Academy and climbs from Slifer Red to legendary duelist. Starts as a lighter school comedy; seasons 3 and 4 go shockingly dark. The most tonally adventurous of the classic era.
media.notes: 180 eps. Season 4 (40 eps) was NEVER dubbed — JP sub only (Crunchyroll). Season 4 is widely considered the best. Do not skip.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Ultimate Masters: World Championship Tournament 2006
media.description: One of the most card-accurate GBA simulators despite releasing on GBA after the DS was already current. Often considered the peak GBA card sim.
media.notes: Core: mgba

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! GX: Tag Force
media.description: First PSP Tag Force game. Partner with GX anime characters in 2-on-2 Tag Duels. Social sim elements: build friendship by gifting food.
media.notes: Core: PPSSPP. The Tag Force series is the most story-rich Yu-Gi-Oh! game line.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! GX: Spirit Caller
media.description: DS GX-era dueling. Enroll at Duel Academy, duel spirits haunting the island. Good touchscreen interface.
media.notes: Core: melonds

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! World Championship 2007
media.description: DS World Championship entry with GX-era cards. Minimal story, strong TCG sim focus.
media.notes: Core: melonds

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! GX: Tag Force 2
media.description: Second PSP Tag Force with GX Season 2 story content. More partners, more cards, more hours. Expanded over TF1 in every dimension.
media.notes: Core: PPSSPP

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! GX: Card Almanac
media.description: Digital card encyclopedia DS app — no gameplay. A reference database and glossary. Japan only.
media.notes: Japan only. Not a real game — it's a card database app. Skip.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! GX: World Championship 2008
media.description: Final GX-era DS World Championship entry. Full GX cast with a current card pool.
media.notes: Core: melonds

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! GX: The Beginning of Destiny
media.description: PS2 GX compilation — essentially Tag Force 1 enhanced. Japan/Europe only (as GX Tag Force Evolution in EU).
media.notes: Japan/EU only. PCSX2 required.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! GX: Tag Force 3
media.description: Final GX Tag Force entry. Season 3 story content, complete GX cast. Never released in North America.
media.notes: EU/JP only. Core: PPSSPP. Fan translation patch available.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! 5D's
media.description: Yusei Fudo races on a Duel Runner in a dystopian future New Domino City. Turbo Duels, Signer Dragons, Dark Signers, and a mythology-heavy story. Widely considered the most story-complete spin-off.
media.notes: 154 eps. EN dub incomplete — cuts off mid-final arc. Watch JP sub on Crunchyroll for the full series.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! 5D's Stardust Accelerator: World Championship 2009
media.description: First 5D's DS game. Parallel story alongside the early anime arcs. Introduces Synchro Monsters to the game system.
media.notes: Core: melonds

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! 5D's: Wheelie Breakers
media.description: Wii kart racer where you play trap cards and summon monsters mid-race. Each racer has 4000 LP instead of a health bar. Insane spinoff with genuine charm.
media.notes: Wii only. Requires Dolphin emulator. Oddball spinoff — surprisingly worth a look.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! 5D's Mobile
media.description: Japanese feature-phone (i-mode / EZweb / Yahoo! Mobile) card game. Tied to the 5D's series. Only ever released in Japan, subsequently delisted from all platforms.
media.notes: DEFUNCT — Japan-only feature phone. Permanently inaccessible.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! 5D's: Tag Force 4
media.description: First 5D's Tag Force entry. Partner with 5D's characters through the Dark Signers arc. Synchro Monsters integrated.
media.notes: Core: PPSSPP

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! The Movie: Bonds Beyond Time
media.description: 10th anniversary crossover film: Yugi, Jaden, and Yusei team up against time-traveling villain Paradox who is erasing the history of Duel Monsters.
media.notes: Watch after completing Duel Monsters, GX, and starting 5D's. Crunchyroll.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! 5D's World Championship 2010: Reverse of Arcadia
media.description: Second 5D's DS World Championship entry covering the Aerial Fortress Seibal arc with an expanded Synchro-era card pool.
media.notes: Core: melonds

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! 5D's: Tag Force 5
media.description: One of the most expansive Tag Force entries — 4,500+ cards and 300+ hours of content. Covers the WRGP arc. Often cited as the best Tag Force game.
media.notes: Core: PPSSPP

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! 5D's: Decade Duels
media.description: XBLA/PSN digital-only arcade card battler with online multiplayer. Followed by a Plus update. Online servers are now gone.
media.notes: Digital only. No longer purchasable. Offline only via preserved backup.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! 5D's: Master of the Cards
media.description: Wii card game using motion controls. Europe only release.
media.notes: EU only. Requires Dolphin emulator.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! 5D's World Championship 2011: Over the Nexus
media.description: Final DS 5D's entry. Custom protagonist, original story alongside the WRGP arc. Generally considered the best 5D's DS game.
media.notes: Core: melonds

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! 5D's: Tag Force 6
media.description: Final 5D's Tag Force entry. Released in Japan only via PSN (no physical). Covers the WRGP arc through to the finale.
media.notes: Japan PSN only. Core: PPSSPP. Fan translation patch available.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Zexal
media.description: Yuma Tsukumo and alien spirit Astral collect the 99 Number cards. Introduces Xyz Summoning. Zexal II (the final arc) is generally considered the peak of the series.
media.notes: 146 eps total (Zexal I + II). The only post-Duel Monsters series with a complete English dub. Crunchyroll.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Zexal World Duel Carnival
media.description: 3DS Zexal-era dueling game. Duel characters from the anime in a tournament bracket structure. Xyz Summoning supported.
media.notes: Core: Citra

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Arc-V
media.description: Yuya Sakaki discovers Pendulum Summoning and gets entangled in a multiverse-spanning war involving parallel versions of himself. Incorporates callbacks to all four summoning methods from prior series.
media.notes: 148 eps. EN dub available but incomplete (JP preferred). Crunchyroll has JP sub.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Millennium Duels
media.description: Sequel to Decade Duels on XBLA/PSN. Covers Duel Monsters-era characters with a larger card pool.
media.notes: Digital only. No longer purchasable on storefronts.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Duel Arena
media.description: Free-to-play browser/PC online card game developed by Miniclip. Servers closed in 2016 — lasted under two years.
media.notes: DEFUNCT — servers shut down 2016. Historical interest only.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Duel Generation
media.description: First official Yu-Gi-Oh! mobile game for iOS and Android. Campaign mode through the anime arcs plus online PvP.
media.notes: DEFUNCT — removed from app stores circa 2019. No longer accessible.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Arc-V Tag Force Special
media.description: Final Tag Force game and the last PSP release. Japan only. Enormous cast featuring characters from every series through Arc-V.
media.notes: Japan only. Core: PPSSPP. Partial fan translation available.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Legacy of the Duelist
media.description: Console/PC card sim covering story arcs from Duel Monsters through Arc-V. Reverse duels let you play as the villain. Superseded by Link Evolution.
media.notes: Superseded by Link Evolution — play LE instead unless stuck on PS4.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Saikyo Card Battle
media.description: 3DS eShop simple card battler with chibi art style. Aimed at younger audiences. Japan only — eShop now closed.
media.notes: Japan only. 3DS eShop closed. No longer accessible officially.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! The Dark Side of Dimensions
media.description: Canonical epilogue to the original Duel Monsters series. New villain Aigami challenges Yugi and Kaiba. Made with Kazuki Takahashi's direct involvement — his final major creative work on the franchise.
media.notes: Watch after completing all of original Duel Monsters. Most streaming platforms.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Duel Links
media.description: The mobile breakthrough: Speed Duels with reduced LP and 3-monster-zone field. All anime series represented with iconic characters and their Skill abilities. Konami's most successful mobile title by far.
media.notes: Free-to-play. iOS/Android/Steam. Still actively updated. Best mobile YGO for story and nostalgia fans.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! VRAINS
media.description: Yusaku Fujiki (Playmaker) exposes a conspiracy in LINK VRAINS, a VR dueling network. Introduces Link Summoning. Cyberpunk aesthetic; the most divisive modern YGO series with fans.
media.notes: 120 eps. JP sub on Crunchyroll. EN dub on Pluto TV (incomplete). Last Gallop-produced series.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Legacy of the Duelist: Link Evolution
media.description: Expanded version of Legacy of the Duelist adding VRAINS content and Link Summoning. 150+ iconic duelists, story coverage from Duel Monsters through VRAINS. The best console story retrospective.
media.notes: Switch/PS4/Xbox/Steam. Best starting point for console newcomers.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Sevens
media.description: Yuga Ohdo invents Rush Duels and fights to free dueling from the authoritarian Goha Corporation. First Bridge-produced (not Gallop) series, lighter tone for younger audiences.
media.notes: 92 eps. Aired on Disney XD in US — not on Crunchyroll. Rush Duel format (draw to 5 each turn).

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Rush Duel: Dawn of the Battle Royale
media.description: Switch Rush Duel game based on the Sevens anime. Simplified Rush Duel format — draw to 5 each turn. Single-player campaign plus local and online multiplayer.
media.notes: Switch only. Rush Duel format only — not standard TCG rules.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Master Duel
media.description: The definitive digital TCG experience. Full official rules, 10,000+ cards, competitive online PvP, card animations, and a solo story mode covering the Duel Monsters manga lore.
media.notes: Free-to-play. All platforms including iOS/Android. The current-meta competitive experience.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Cross Duel
media.description: 4-player simultaneous mobile dueling: four players on a single field, each sending monsters in four directions to attack all three opponents at once. Terminated after exactly one year.
media.notes: DEFUNCT — servers terminated September 4, 2023.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Go Rush!!
media.description: Sequel to Sevens. Alien Yudias Velgear comes to Earth to learn Rush Dueling. Yuga and friends appear. Lighter comedy tone with sci-fi elements.
media.notes: Watch Sevens first. ~100 eps. Limited international streaming — fansubs primary option.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! RUSH DUEL: Saikyo Battle Royale Let's Go Go Rush!!
media.description: Go Rush!! tie-in Switch game. Expanded version of Dawn of the Battle Royale with Go Rush!! characters and cards added.
media.notes: Japan only. Switch. Rush Duel format.

media.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! Early Days Collection
media.description: Definitive compilation of 14 classic Game Boy/GBA games (1998–2004), including several never released outside Japan. Quality-of-life enhancements, save states, and online battle support.
media.notes: Switch/Steam (February 2025). The best legal way to experience the classic handheld era.
